text.title: Bug Pattern to Lint Rule Conversion Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for converting a recurring bug into a deterministic detector. The decision-record validator enforces them.

rule.statement: A pattern qualifies for conversion only when ≥3 distinct ticket instances exist within 30 days (OR ≥5 in 90 days). Single-instance issues stay as ad-hoc fixes; the conversion overhead is wasted below threshold.
rule.rationale: Maintaining a lint rule has steady-state cost (false positives, exception lists). The cost only pays off when the pattern recurs.

rule.statement: The detector (ruff rule id, ESLint rule, regex, AST visitor, test name) MUST be drafted and tested against the bug-sample BEFORE the corrective fix is written. Recording a "we should not do X" without a detector creates a rule with no enforcement.
rule.rationale: Writing the fix first creates rules with no detector; readers skim, agents skip. The detector enforces going forward; the fix only patches the past.

rule.statement: The decision-record names a single accountable owner by email (not "the team"). The owner is responsible for the rule's false-positive triage and quarterly review.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; the rule rots when it fires falsely with no one to triage.

rule.statement: Before wiring into pre-commit, the detector is run against the last 90 days of code. If it fires on more than 5% of files OR contains >5% false-positives within the cluster, the pattern is refined (or rejected) before adoption.
rule.rationale: Above 5% FP, contributors learn to ignore the rule; below 5% they trust it.

rule.statement: The decision-record carries semver version + last_reviewed date. Quarterly review re-checks: is the rule still firing? Is the original cluster still recurring? Stale records (>90 days unreviewed) are flagged on read.
rule.rationale: Source code evolves; detectors lag. Forced review cadence catches dead rules.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (cluster identified, lint infra present, owner assignable), execute: cluster-tickets → draft-detector → FP-scan → wire-into-pre-commit → emit-record. Do not skip the FP-scan.
rule.rationale: Adoption-by-vibe rules get silenced within a week; adoption-by-FP-measurement rules stay.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) pattern is one-off, (b) detector requires runtime tracing — use observability instead, (c) FP > 5% even after refinement, (d) team has no lint infrastructure — bootstrap `ci-quality-gate-design` first.
rule.rationale: Conversion overhead wasted without recurrence, lint substrate, or mechanical detector.
